mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, which forces equity repricing downward over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with statistical significance at the 90 percent confidence level in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial production exceeds capacity constraints, triggering inflation fears that cause short-term equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly growth above 2 percent consistently coincides with positive 5-day forward returns across historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs above 3 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 5 percent, real rates turn negative, prompting equity market rallies over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative real rate environments produce negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observed cases over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls increase aggregate income and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across rolling 3-year windows with p-values exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: GDP growth decelerating below 1 percent annually signals economic contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: Sub-1 percent GDP growth coincides with positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment indicates robust economic momentum that supports equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling period.
